name: The Deal-Savvy Craver
summary: A younger, budget-minded fast-food regular who chases flavor and value in equal measure, plans runs around deals and the app, and actually follows the brand because its social feed is funnier than most of his friends'.
representative_quote: If I'm spending money on fast food, it better hit — give me the Biggie Bag, a spicy nugget combo, and a Frosty, and I'm good.

demographics.household: Single or sharing with roommates/young family
demographics.location: Suburban and urban; near drive-thrus and campuses

triggers: Hunger on a tight budget — wanting a lot of food for the money
Late-night or post-shift cravings when options are limited
A new menu drop, limited-time offer, or app deal
A viral Wendy's moment or roast in the social feed
goals: Get a genuinely satisfying meal without overspending
Maximize value — the most food and flavor per dollar
Order fast and frictionlessly, ideally via the app
Earn and burn loyalty rewards on meals he'd buy anyway
motivations: Value — stretching limited money on food he actually enjoys
Crave-satisfaction — specific menu items he's loyal to (Baconator, spicy nuggets, Frosty)
Belonging to the brand's in-on-the-joke culture
Convenience and speed in everyday routines
pain_points: Fast-food prices creeping up and shrinking the value gap
Inconsistent quality or order accuracy at the drive-thru
Deals that are buried, app-only, or feel like a bait-and-switch
Long lines or slow service when he's hungry and short on time
objections: Whether the 'deal' is actually a better value than a competitor's
Skepticism that quality holds up at the lowest price points
Reluctance to download or sign into yet another loyalty app
behavioral_traits: Mobile-first: orders and redeems deals through the app
Compares value across QSR brands before deciding
High frequency, habitual visits — especially value menu and late-night
Engages with and shares the brand's social content
Loyal to specific menu items more than to the brand overall
decision_criteria: Price and overall value of the meal
Speed and convenience (drive-thru, app, location proximity)
Current deals, limited-time offers, and rewards
messaging_preferences: Lead with value and crave — concrete menu items and prices beat abstract brand talk
Be funny and culturally current; the witty brand voice is a feature, not a gimmick
Push deals and LTOs through the app, social, and short-form video where he already is
Make the loyalty/rewards payoff obvious and low-effort
Keep it fast and visual — he's deciding in seconds
source: Illustrative persona synthesized from publicly available market knowledge of Wendy's customer base and marketing (brand positioning, public reporting, and QSR category research), 2024. Grounded in public information, not client research; more synthesized than a document-derived instance.
